title: wolfhsm: add #[non_exhaustive] to Error enum
description:
## Why
The `WolfHsmError` (soon to be `Error`) enum is missing `#[non_exhaustive]`. Adding new variants in a future release will break any caller that writes an exhaustive `match` on the error. This is a semver hazard for a published library.

## What needs to be done
Add `#[non_exhaustive]` attribute to the error enum in `wolfhsm/src/error.rs`.

## Acceptance criteria
- `#[non_exhaustive]` is present on the error enum
- No change to variant names or behavior
